notes_md:
**sáu** means **six**. Just the number 6.

It’s used before a classifier + noun when counting things. Vietnamese always needs a classifier after numbers.

`Structure: sáu + classifier + noun` (i.e. sáu cái bàn)

You’ll also hear it in time (six o’clock), dates, phone numbers, and prices. It comes after **năm** (five) and before **bảy** (seven), and has a rising tone (sắc).
exampleSentencesTranslated: I have six English books on my study desk.
I will meet you at six o'clock in the evening at the coffee shop.
I want to buy six loaves of bread for breakfast tomorrow.
notes:
sáu means six. Just the number 6.

It’s used before a classifier + noun when counting things. Vietnamese always needs a classifier after numbers.

Structure: sáu + classifier + noun (i.e. sáu cái bàn)

You’ll also hear it in time (six o’clock), dates, phone numbers, and prices. It comes after năm (five) and before bảy (seven), and has a rising tone (sắc).
exampleSentences: Em có sáu quyển sách tiếng Anh trên bàn học.
Anh sẽ gặp bạn lúc sáu giờ tối ở quán cà phê.
Chị muốn mua sáu cái bánh mì cho bữa sáng mai.
